latestPosts.title: People, process, technology, business
latestPosts.summary: Things leaders should keep in their radar
latestPosts.content:
I just watched [this presentation](https://www.annashipman.co.uk/jfdi/people-process-tech-business.html) from Anna Shipman:
Alex Ewerlöf presents it as [t-pop](https://blog.alexewerlof.com/p/t-pop) framework:
[![T-Pop](/assets/posts/2025-08-05-4-pillars/t-pop.webp)](https://blog.alexewerlof.com/p/t-pop)
And this is also how Patrick Kua defines the [engineering manager archetypes](https://www.patkua.com/blog/5-engineering-manager-archetypes/):
[![engineering manager archetypes](/assets/posts/2025-08-05-4-pillars/patkua.png)](https://www.patkua.com/blog/5-engineering-manager-archetypes/)
When I started as [a manager in 2019](https://www.linkedin.com/in/adamatti/), one mentor explained to me those 4 pillars. You can't focus in just one or two to grow as manager. Neither as tech lead or software engineer. I used it a lot to interview people and conduce team retrospectives.

latestPosts.title: The Tiger, the lion and the panther are harmless animals.
latestPosts.content: > The Tiger, the lion and the panther are harmless animals. Instead, chickens, geese and ducks, they are highly dangerous animals  – a worm said to his children - Bertrand Russell
